pr.title: ft: improve Crazy Hand init command dispatch
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Closed, unmerged PR that attempted to improve Crazy Hand's human init command dispatcher, especially ftCh_Init_80156AD8. The patch replaced a small btn_pressed inline helper with a local buttons macro and cached L-button mask, corrected the apparent A/Z command mapping, used named float data symbols for zero and two constants, and added extern declarations for those constants in ftCh_Init.h. The PR reported improved but still imperfect checkdiff matching at 98.99317%, with remaining drift attributed to pad-word register allocation and derived mask temporaries. A later comment stated the work was already done elsewhere, which likely explains the closed-unmerged state.
what_changed: In ftCh_Init_80156AD8, removed the static inline btn_pressed(u32 mask) helper that returned HSD_PadMasterStatus[3].button & mask.
Introduced a temporary preprocessor alias, #define buttons (HSD_PadMasterStatus[3].button), around the dispatcher body and #undef buttons afterward.
Cached the L-button test once as u32 l_pressed = buttons & HSD_PAD_L and reused it for the L + D-pad command cases.
Rewrote all command-condition checks to directly test buttons & HSD_PAD_* masks instead of calling btn_pressed repeatedly.
Changed several Vec3 z assignments from literal 0 to the named float symbol ftCh_Init_804DA05C.
Changed random-range scaling from literal 2 to the named float symbol ftCh_Init_804DA060 in the B + D-pad-up random position setup.

Swapped the dispatcher mapping for A and Z command groups: the BackAirplane2/BackAirplane3 cases moved from HSD_PAD_A to HSD_PAD_Z, while ftCh_Init_801597F0 and ftCh_Init_80159F40 cases moved from HSD_PAD_Z to HSD_PAD_A.
Added extern f32 declarations for ftCh_Init_804DA05C and ftCh_Init_804DA060 to ftCh_Init.h.
smart_moves: Matched the style of the analogous Master Hand dispatcher by caching the L-button mask separately, according to the PR body.

Used named data symbols for recurring float constants instead of immediates, making the generated code and data references closer to the target and documenting that the values are file-level constants.
Localized the buttons macro with a matching #undef, avoiding permanent preprocessor pollution outside ftCh_Init_80156AD8.
Identified the remaining nonmatch cause narrowly as register allocation around the full pad word and mask temporaries, which is useful for future matching attempts.
Verified both targeted diff quality and full build: checkdiff for ftCh_Init_80156AD8 reached 98.99317%, and configure.py plus ninja completed successfully.
decomp_lessons: For dispatcher-heavy input code, small expression-shape changes such as direct mask tests versus helper calls can materially affect register allocation and matching.

When a sibling implementation is already matched or closer to matched, mirroring its local caching pattern can be a strong tactic; here the PR explicitly mirrored the matched Master Hand dispatcher style.
Named float constants should be preferred when the original code appears to load from static float data, especially for common values like 0.0f and 2.0f used in Vec3 setup or random range math.
Command dispatch tables or long if/else chains should be checked carefully for controller-button mapping mistakes; this patch corrected A/Z behavior in the Crazy Hand init command dispatcher.
A high checkdiff percentage does not imply byte match; document the specific residual drift, especially when it is isolated to register allocation.
Closed-unmerged PRs can still contain useful tactics, but future work should verify whether equivalent changes landed elsewhere before reapplying.
naming_conventions: Crazy Hand init symbols use the ftCh_Init_ prefix, for example ftCh_Init_80156AD8, ftCh_Init_804DA05C, and ftCh_Init_804DA060.

Unknown file/static functions in this area still use address-derived names such as fn_80157080, fn_80156F6C, fn_8015746C, fn_8015755C, fn_801578E8, fn_80157C50, fn_80158144, fn_801582D8, fn_801587B0, fn_80159908, fn_80159AA4, and fn_8015AAC8.
Crazy Hand fighter variable access follows fp->fv.ch fields, including fp->fv.ch.x2250 for Master Hand/Crazy Hand motion-state-like values.
DatAttrs fields remain offset-based or partially typed names such as x24, x20, x30_pos2, x88_pos, x110_pos, and x118_pos.
Pad masks follow HSD_PAD_* names, including HSD_PAD_L, HSD_PAD_R, HSD_PAD_A, HSD_PAD_B, HSD_PAD_Z, HSD_PAD_Y, and HSD_PAD_DPAD*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace inline helper calls with direct macro-expanded access to HSD_PadMasterStatus[3].button when trying to influence register allocation in input dispatch code.
Cache repeated button-mask results, as with u32 l_pressed = buttons & HSD_PAD_L, to match sibling functions and reduce repeated load/test expression variation.
Use externally declared float symbols for constants when the target appears to load constants from .sdata/.rodata rather than materializing literals.
Keep condition order and else-if structure stable while adjusting expression form; this PR targeted codegen shape without broad control-flow restructuring.
Remaining drift was reported as the target using r0 for the full pad word while the current C generated r5, with related derived mask temp register differences.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.interpretation: The PR was likely closed because equivalent or overlapping work had already been completed elsewhere. No detailed code review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find the PR or commit by jellejurre that already implemented the Crazy Hand init dispatcher improvements.
Check current master for ftCh_Init_80156AD8 to see whether the A/Z mapping, l_pressed caching, and ftCh_Init_804DA05C/804DA060 constants landed elsewhere.
Compare Crazy Hand ftCh_Init_80156AD8 against the matched Master Hand dispatcher to reuse the same input-mask expression patterns.
Investigate remaining checkdiff drift in ftCh_Init_80156AD8 related to full pad-word register allocation r0 versus r5.
